findings: Backward bulk and flag features (e23) exhibit extremely low cardinality and near-constant values, indicating low diversity.

Some backward packet length features (e9) also show low cardinality and near-constant behavior, while others have class-conditioned structural variation, confirming coexistence of stable and variable feature subsets.
This coexistence preserves the documented tension between low diversity and class-conditioned variation within the local feature subsets.
contradictions: The presence of duplicated rows conflicts with observed class-conditioned structural variation and strong feature dependencies, complicating interpretation of feature stability.
limitations: Distribution verification for class-conditioned variation was not performed due to budget exhaustion, limiting deeper quantification of variation profiles.
